description_long: This is a wilted or dried flower with large, drooping petals that are a faded tan or brown color, suggesting it is past its prime bloom. The petals are broad, somewhat wrinkled, and have a papery texture, with some overlapping and curling at the edges. The flower is supported by a sturdy green stem that is upright and segmented, with several elongated, lanceolate green leaves emerging at intervals along its length. The leaves are narrow, pointed, and arch away from the stem, giving the plant a slightly wild and unkempt appearance. The flower head is composed of several petals radiating from a central point, but the overall symmetry is somewhat irregular due to the wilted state. This plant would likely be found in an urban park, roadside, or neglected garden, adding a touch of natural decay to the environment.
description: A wilted, tan-brown flower with broad, drooping petals and a sturdy green stem with narrow, pointed leaves. The plant has a slightly wild, unkempt look, suggesting it is past its prime bloom.
description_view_0: Front view showing three large, drooping tan petals and a green stem with several narrow leaves radiating outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the downward curve of the petals and the arching shape of the leaves along the stem.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the petals and the full spread of the lower leaves, with the stem clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the wilted, curled nature of the petals and the layered arrangement of the leaves.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a somewhat radial pattern around the central axis, but the symmetry is irregular due to wilting. There is some overlapping and curling, with petals drooping downward and partially covering each other.
